page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.large is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 physical cores (2 vCPUs) and 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and its baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1078.28 is categorized as weak (bottom 25%), while the multi-core score of 2088.79 falls into the poor category (bottom 10%). Memory bandwidth tests show 15.52 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 13.96 GB/sec for large-block write operations, both registering in the bottom 10%. Database and key-value operations, such as Redis SET, achieve 2.37 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is limited to 1.54 GB/sec.

As a burstable instance, the ecs.t5-c1m1.large is designed for budget-conscious scenarios where CPU utilization remains low on average but requires occasional bursts. The primary tradeoff is the CPU credit system; sustained high CPU utilization will exhaust credits, leading to throttled performance. Given the low memory capacity of 2.0 GB and the lack of local storage, this instance is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads. Appropriate use cases include low-traffic web servers, microservices, development or testing environments, and basic automation scripts that do not require continuous high-performance compute or memory resources.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.large is a burstable, entry-level virtual server designed for budget-sensitive workloads with low baseline CPU utilization. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, and memory bandwidth constrained to 15.52 GB/sec for large reads. This instance utilizes a burstable CPU credit model, making it highly cost-effective for idle or low-utilization tasks but unsuitable for sustained heavy compute. It is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.large is a burstable x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 2 GB RAM. It is designed for low-cost, low-utilization workloads like development environments and basic web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m1.large is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 2 GB RAM, optimized for low-cost, low-utilization workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable virtual server designed for low-utilization workloads, development environments, and lightweight background tasks.

Burstable CPU allocation model for cost-effective, intermittent workloads
No local storage or dedicated GPU resources included
Suitable for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and testing environments
Low baseline performance profile optimized for budget efficiency
